data.name: waveLengthControl.ToolTip
data.value: Incident electron energy (accelerating voltage) in keV. It sets the beam energy used for the trajectory, cross-section, mean-free-path and stopping-power calculation; higher energy gives deeper penetration.

data.name: buttonSimulate.ToolTip
data.value: Run the Monte-Carlo simulation: trace the set number of incident electrons through the sample, then update the statistics, distribution graphs and 3-D trajectory view.

data.name: numericBoxSampleTilt.ToolTip
data.value: Sample surface tilt relative to the incident beam (0 to -90 deg). 0 deg = surface normal along the beam; tilting changes the backscattered-electron yield and direction distribution.

data.name: buttonViewFromX.ToolTip
data.value: Set the 3-D view to look along the X-axis, which is the sample rotation (tilt) axis.

data.name: buttonViewFromZ.ToolTip
data.value: Set the 3-D view to look along the Z-axis, i.e. down the incident beam direction.

data.name: numericBoxCalcNum.ToolTip
data.value: Number of electron trajectories to simulate (100 to 1,000,000). More trajectories give smoother statistics but take longer to compute.

data.name: numericBoxDrawNum.ToolTip
data.value: Maximum number of trajectories to render in the 3-D view (1 to 100,000). This only limits drawing, not the simulation; lower values keep the 3-D view responsive.

data.name: checkBoxDrawAxes.ToolTip
data.value: Show the X (red), Y (green) and Z = beam direction (purple) coordinate axes in the 3-D trajectory view.

data.name: checkBoxDrawGuidCircles.ToolTip
data.value: Draw concentric scale circles on the sample surface in the 3-D view as a distance reference (spacing auto-scaled, labelled in nm/µm).

data.name: buttonSurfaceNormal.ToolTip
data.value: Set the 3-D view to look along the sample surface normal (accounting for the current tilt).

data.name: checkBoxDrawAbsorved.Text
data.value: Draw trajectories absorbed in the sample

data.name: checkBoxDrawAbsorved.ToolTip
data.value: Also draw the trajectories of electrons absorbed in the sample (those that did not backscatter), not just the backscattered ones, in the 3-D view.

data.name: radioButtonStandardDeviation.ToolTip
data.value: Colour the stereographic projection by the standard deviation of the backscattered electrons' exit energy in each direction.

data.name: radioButtonAverageEnergy.ToolTip
data.value: Colour the stereographic projection by the average exit energy (keV) of the backscattered electrons in each direction.

data.name: radioButtonFrequency.ToolTip
data.value: Colour the stereographic projection by frequency: the number of backscattered electrons exiting in each direction.

data.name: checkBoxDrawAxesInStereonet.ToolTip
data.value: Overlay the ±X / ±Y / ±Z axis positions on the stereographic projection (pole figure) of the backscattered-electron exit directions.

data.name: labelBSEenergy.ToolTip
data.value: Average exit energy of the backscattered electrons (BSE) leaving the sample surface, in keV.

data.name: labelStoppingPower.ToolTip
data.value: Stopping power dE/ds: energy the electron loses per unit path length to inelastic collisions, at the incident energy (eV/nm).

data.name: labelCrossSection.ToolTip
labelMeanFreePath.ToolTip
data.value: Elastic mean free path λ: average distance the electron travels between elastic scattering events, at the incident energy (nm).

data.name: labelBSEratio.ToolTip
data.value: Backscatter coefficient η: fraction of incident electrons re-emitted from the surface as backscattered electrons (BSE), from the Monte-Carlo simulation.

data.name: label3.ToolTip
data.value: Average exit energy of the backscattered electrons (BSE) leaving the sample surface, in keV.

data.name: label6.ToolTip
label5.ToolTip
data.value: Stopping power dE/ds: energy the electron loses per unit path length to inelastic collisions, at the incident energy (eV/nm).

data.name: label4.ToolTip
data.value: Elastic mean free path λ: average distance the electron travels between elastic scattering events, at the incident energy (nm).

data.name: label2.ToolTip
data.value: Backscatter coefficient η: fraction of incident electrons re-emitted from the surface as backscattered electrons (BSE), from the Monte-Carlo simulation.

data.name: checkBoxDrawPathAfterEscape.ToolTip
data.value: Extend each backscattered electron's path beyond the surface to show its escape direction in the 3-D view (coloured by exit energy).

data.name: poleFigureControl.ToolTip
data.value: The stereonet center corresponds to the surface-normal direction.

data.name: toolStripStatusLabel1.ToolTipText
data.value: Status bar: shows the elapsed time for the trajectory calculation, graph drawing and 3-D rendering of the last simulation.
